free_text: Neutron-induced 235U fission spectrum measurements
using liquid organic scintillation detectors
Measurement performed using Weapons
Neutron Research (WNR) beam at 30. right at the Los
Alamos Neutron Science Center LANSCE.
235U deposited on each side of 10 thin foils in PPAC
chamber. Total mass of the 235U was 113 mg with
target spot of 4 cm in diameter.
Parallel-plate avalanche counter with internal
target containing 235U was used for detection of
fission fragments. It was located at a distance of
22.675 m from the spallation target. Its time
resolution was approx. 1 ns.
Neutrons were detected using five EJ-309
cylindrical liquid scintillation detectors, with a
depth of 13.3 cm and diameter of 13 cm. Detectors
were placed at a distance of 80 cm to the center of
fission counter. EJ-309 liquid scintillator has a
short light-emission component of 3.5 ns, which
results in excellent timing properties and pulse shape
discrimination.
Energy of the primary incident neutrons was
determined using time of flight over a distance of
22.675 m from the spallation target to the fission
chamber. Energy of secondary neutrons was determined
using time of flight between fission chamber and
neutron detector.
Neutrons were produced by spallation reaction
using 800 MeV protons from linear accelerator
impinging on a tungsten target.
Compiled by S.H.
Data presented in fig. 9 of the reference
sent by author A.E.
Total uncertainty determined from
- statistical uncertainty
- systematic uncertainty in detector efficiency
- systematic uncertainty in particle identification
- systematic uncertainty in flight path
